target: raw-text/08-live-power.txt (new archive file) + SEC-3.1 verifiability footnote in data/sections.json part3
curmudgeon_review: N/A — triggered by ISS-074 (archive Live Power page for verifiability)
archive_description: Complete archive of the dome's Live Power Dashboard page (live.html) as of 2026-04-07. Captures both the static HTML structure (20 domain descriptions, formulas, falsification thresholds, correlation matrix, 'How This Works' section, AI Auditor checklist) and a dynamic data snapshot from status_history.json (2026-04-05T04:40:56Z). Includes 7 notable observations for our review: pred=obs identity checks, tolerance gaming, tautological domains, contradicted independence claims, σ inflation (9.2σ static vs 14.26σ dynamic), unscored domain exclusion, and unfalsifiable thresholds.
sec31_footnote_location: Insert after the first paragraph of Section 3.1 (after 'There is no out-of-sample prediction.')
sec31_footnote_html: Verifiability note: Our 20-domain classification below is based on the dome's Live Power page (live.html) as archived on 7 April 2026, with dynamic data from status_history.json timestamped 5 April 2026. The full page content — including per-domain formulas, falsification thresholds, claimed tolerances, and the cross-domain correlation matrix — is preserved in our repository at raw-text/08-live-power.txt. Readers can compare our classification against the dome's own published descriptions to verify that we have not mischaracterized any domain.

additional_findings.sigma_discrepancy: The Live Power page displays two different significance values: the static 'Audit Statistics' box shows 9.2σ (the number cited in the page header and our review), but the dynamic status_history.json computes 14.26σ via Fisher's method across 19 domains. This is a 55% disagreement between the same page's static and dynamic content. Our SEC-3.1 currently cites only 9.2σ, which is the more conservative (and therefore more charitable) number.
additional_findings.pred_equals_obs_pattern: At least 3 domains in the snapshot have predicted=observed exactly (Schumann 7.83=7.83, SAA 77.0=77.0, Polaris 0.27°=0.27°). This corroborates our Part 4.6 code analysis finding that monitor.py uses hardcoded pred=obs identity checks for many domains.
additional_findings.adaptive_tolerance: NMP Drift Rate now uses 50% tolerance (confirmed in snapshot: 39.9% error scored as PASS). Our SEC-3.1 mentions adaptive tolerances but doesn't cite this specific number. The 50% threshold means the prediction could be off by half and still 'pass.'
additional_findings.unscored_domains: 6 of the claimed 20 domains are unscored/pending (Eclipse 2026, Kp Index, Schumann Amplitude Suppression, AAO, Polaris multi-lat, Aetheric Slipstream). The 100% score is computed only over the 14 scored domains. This is relevant to our denominator analysis in Section 3.5.6.
additional_findings.falsification_impossibility: Several falsification thresholds are set to conditions that have never occurred in recorded history — e.g., Schumann frequency drifting >0.3 Hz for >1 hour. The Schumann fundamental has been stable at 7.83 ± 0.05 Hz since its discovery in 1960. Setting the falsification bar at 6× the observed variation range means the test cannot fail.

kernel_of_truth: The dome model's Live Power Dashboard represents a genuine attempt at falsifiable, transparent science. The falsification thresholds for each domain, the automated pipeline, the Bitcoin timestamping, and the AI Auditor checklist are structurally sound practices that most alternative cosmologies don't attempt. Credit is due for making the monitoring infrastructure public and auditable. The problem is not the infrastructure — it's that the constants being monitored were fitted to the data being observed, the tolerances are set wide enough to guarantee pass, and many 'observations' are hardcoded to equal the 'predictions.'
holes_addressed: ISS-074: Live Power page now archived for reproducibility
Readers can verify our 20-domain classification against the dome's own descriptions
Date-stamped snapshot prevents 'you analyzed an old version' objection
anticipated_objections: Dome author may update Live Power page after seeing our archive — the dated snapshot documents the version we analyzed
'Our domains have changed since your archive' — the git history at john09289/predictions preserves all prior versions; our archive captures the state at analysis time
'You didn't include the dynamic chart data' — status_history.json snapshot is included with timestamp
